plan_summary: GradientBoosting with re-tuned hyperparameters (max_depth=3, 300 estimators, lr=0.05) and HasCabin feature for Titanic binary classification
reviewer_reasoning: Iteration 5 degrades the primary metric for the second consecutive iteration (iter 3: 0.8445 → iter 4: 0.8406 → iter 5: 0.8387). Medium overfitting persists (8.62% train/val gap). No high-severity flags or leakage. Trend is plateau/declining from the best at iteration 3. Five iterations remain and three untested strategy classes (XGBoost/LightGBM, SVM, ensemble stacking) offer clear headroom. Continued refinement of GBM in this hyperparameter neighbourhood is unlikely to recover ground; a different algorithm class is warranted.
router_reasoning: Iterations 4 and 5 both regressed vs iteration 3 (the best at AUC 0.8445). Regression is caused by approach limitations, not a code bug — executions succeeded cleanly. Rolling back to iteration 3 as the planning base. Next plan should pivot to XGBoost or LightGBM: gradient-boosted trees with modern regularisation (L1/L2, subsampling, colsample) that better control the train/val gap. Engineered features (Title, Sex, HasCabin, Fare, Age, FamilySize) are solid and should be retained.

risk_flags_summary.evidence: Train/val AUC-ROC gap of 8.62% (train 0.918 vs val 0.839); learning curve trend: plateau
